videos.title: Type This Into Claude, It'll Make You Build 10x Faster.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese presents six power phrases for prompting Claude Code to accelerate development workflows. The techniques draw from practices used by Claude Code's creator and Andrej Karpathy, with emphasis on structuring projects, scaling processes, and avoiding common mistakes in AI-assisted coding.

videos.title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
videos.summary: IBM Technology discusses how design systems and context engineering guide AI agents in modern software development. MCP and AI agents work together to ensure consistent, correct application building by bridging design systems and code.

videos.title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
videos.summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates how to build a self-improving knowledge base for the Hermes AI agent using Obsidian as a wiki, connected to Claude and NotebookLM. The workflow implements Andrej Karpathy's LLM wiki principles with automatic ingestion of meetings, emails, and research via cron jobs and tools like Granola, creating a persistent memory system that scales beyond the agent's base context window.

videos.title: RIP Claude Fable, open-source AI unleashed, full body avatars, new Google models, new TTS: AI NEWS
videos.summary: AI Search presents a comprehensive news segment covering recent AI models and tools including GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, Claude Fable, MiniMax M3, and various research projects in video generation, avatars, and speech synthesis. The video reviews multiple emerging AI capabilities across language models, multimodal systems, and creative generation tools.

videos.title: Is This The FASTEST AI Model In The World?!! (Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed)
videos.summary: Xiaomi and TileRT released a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model called MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed that achieves over 1,000 tokens per second on standard hardware. The model uses selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime to maximize inference speed.

videos.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
videos.summary: David Ondrej reports that Fable 5 has been banned and frames this as signaling the emergence of a permanent underclass. The video discusses concerns around open-source AI and appears critical of the decision.

videos.title: This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5
videos.summary: A YouTube video reports that Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models were shut down by US government order after only three days of public availability. The commentary raises concerns about whether frontier AI systems will remain accessible to the public or become restricted to government control.

videos.title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
videos.summary: A YouTube video analyzing competitive shifts in the AI industry as GPT-6 development approaches. The creator argues that OpenAI's dominance is eroding as Microsoft, Apple, and Google deploy alternative models in flagship products, and discusses benchmark performance across competing systems.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin analyzes Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model withdrawal following a US government export-control directive allegedly tied to jailbreak concerns. The video evaluates whether Fable 5 lived up to hype relative to Opus 4.8 based on pre-withdrawal benchmarks and discusses implications for developers building on these models.

videos.title: 1,000+ Tokens/Sec: Google Just Shattered the AI Speed Limit (DiffusionGemma)
videos.summary: Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma, a model that applies image-generation diffusion techniques to text generation using uniform state diffusion. The architecture shifts local LLMs from memory-bound to compute-bound, theoretically achieving generation speeds exceeding 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs through bidirectional multi-pass refinement.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: BREAKING: US Government BANNED Mythos and Fable
videos.summary: The US government issued an export control directive that forced Anthropic to immediately disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers. Better Stack reports this development as breaking news via YouTube.

videos.title: Google’s New AI Just Broke The AI Speed Limit: DiffusionGemma
videos.summary: Google released DiffusionGemma, an open AI model that generates text using a novel approach and achieves up to four times faster inference on dedicated GPUs. The model features a 256-token canvas and self-correction capabilities.

videos.title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
videos.summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It provides a flexible ecosystem supporting multiple interfaces including desktop app, IDE, CLI, and SDK for autonomous agent-based development workflows.

videos.title: AIventure: Vibe Coding Journey
videos.summary: AIventure is an open source retro dungeon crawler built on Angular and Phaser.js, powered by Gemma 4, that teaches developers vibe coding and agentic behaviors. It demonstrates local and cloud model serving options including Transformers.js, Ollama, LM Studio, Gemini API, and Google Cloud.

videos.title: PI AGENT FULL COURSE: Master Pi Agent in 30 Minutes
videos.summary: David Ondrej presents a tutorial on Pi Agent, a framework for building coding agents. The course covers practical implementation with support for real coding agent development.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Just Changed Website Design Forever
videos.summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates Claude Fable 5's website design generation capabilities through progressive tests, comparing it against Opus 4.8 and showing its ability to replicate design patterns from reference sites. The video argues Fable 5 represents a significant shift in how AI handles web design tasks, with discussion of token efficiency tradeoffs.

videos.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
videos.summary: Claude FM is a music streaming service designed for focus and productivity during thinking and building work. The playlists are made and curated by musicians.

videos.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
videos.summary: Claude FM is a music streaming service created by Anthropic for thinking and building work. The content is curated by musicians to support focused creative and technical tasks.

videos.title: How to Use Higgsfield AI Like a PRO – AI Marketing Workflow
videos.summary: Higgsfield Supercomputer is an AI super-agent that generates full marketing campaigns from a single idea, producing TV ads, podcast clips, vertical reels, and character-driven shorts with consistency across formats. The tool automates visual content creation and replaces manual shoot-edit workflows, positioning itself as a competitive advantage for marketing automation in 2026.

videos.title: Converting SQL Server code to PostgreSQL
videos.summary: Google Cloud demonstrates using Gemini, an AI assistant, to convert SQL Server T-SQL code to PostgreSQL. The tool helps developers migrate database schemas and explains PostgreSQL concepts to improve their skills in the process.

videos.title: The Rise of Generative UI for Developers (CopilotKit)
videos.summary: CopilotKit is a framework that enables developers to integrate AI agents directly into React and Next.js applications by rendering real UI components and sharing state, moving beyond bolted-on chatbot windows. The tool supports human-in-the-loop approval workflows and generative UI patterns.

videos.title: How I make my AI Agent remember everything
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery shares a technique for implementing persistent memory in AI agents. The video demonstrates how to enable agents to retain and recall information across interactions.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform enables unified data visibility across finance, supply chain, HR, and sales functions. She argues that enterprise-scale planning spreadsheets fail at scale, advocates for building complete platforms rather than single features, and emphasizes hiring discipline and trusted customer validation as keys to competing against established players.

videos.title: The $20,000 API Key Mistake
videos.summary: Keith AI presents a case study of a $20,000 incident involving improper API key handling. The video serves as a warning about the financial risks of security mismanagement in AI API usage.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 & Apple’s NVIDIA deal
videos.summary: A Mixture of Experts podcast episode hosted by IBM Technology debates whether Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 justifies the hype, analyzing its tier routing system as a Mythos-class model. The panel also discusses Apple's shift away from on-device AI to partner with NVIDIA for cloud compute at WWDC 2026, and whether AI models can detect sarcasm using multimodal inputs or context alone.

videos.title: The “Final Boss” of AI Workflows: UltraCode
videos.summary: Keith AI discusses UltraCode as a solution for AI workflows, framing it as an advanced or comprehensive approach to workflow management. The video title suggests UltraCode represents a significant capability in the AI workflow space.

videos.title: Master Claude Code with Claude Fable 5 (57 mins)
videos.summary: Keith AI's 57-minute video demonstrates advanced use of Claude Code as a coding system rather than a chat interface. The video covers Desktop, CLI, and web modes, permissions, connectors, slash commands, plugins, routines, sub-agents, and dynamic workflows for building apps and automating development workflows.

videos.title: Code with Claude Tokyo 2026: Opening Keynote
videos.summary: Anthropic held an opening keynote at Code with Claude 2026 in Tokyo featuring engineering and product leaders. The event provided updates on company developments and strategic direction.

videos.title: The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 faces backlash for refusing harmless prompts, triggering false positives, and limiting AI-related work through unclear guardrails. The controversy centers on trust: users question whether they receive the full model, a downgraded fallback, or a weakened version modified behind the scenes. The piece argues that frontier AI systems are increasingly judged by user trust rather than capability alone.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Yossi Matias on the golden age of research
videos.summary: Yossi Matias, VP of Google Research, discusses the "magic cycle" of research that moves breakthroughs into real-world applications. He covers flood forecasting, MedGemma, Gemini for Science, and tools like Co-Scientist and ERA for scientific discovery. The conversation also touches on speculative decoding, generative UI, and quantum milestones including the Willow chip.

videos.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech explains how to build AI-powered agents using the Agent Development Kit and Model Context Protocol to ground agents in enterprise data via Looker. The tutorial covers MCP configuration, the MCP Toolbox for Databases, tools.yaml setup, API credential management, and creating agents that translate prompts into data requests.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 + YouTube = $30,000/mo
videos.summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates a ZeroEdit video production pipeline using Claude Fable 5 Code to automatically edit YouTube videos. The system deconstructs successful channels, writes scripts, records voiceover, and uses Claude as an editing engine integrated with Higsfield CLI for full video assembly at approximately $2 per video.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace where non-technical users can ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion without SQL knowledge. The LLM generates reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cost-effective.

videos.title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
videos.summary: Cursor's Sam Whitmore discusses scaling and coordinating large numbers of coding agents with Baseten leaders. The conversation covers running agents at scale, agent-to-agent communication and review, and shifting from task parallelization toward system-level design.

videos.title: What Should An AI Device Look Like? — With Alex Himel
videos.summary: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, discusses why Meta believes smart glasses could become the next major computing platform for AI assistants. The conversation covers potential use cases like meetings, reminders, and fitness tracking, on-device AI, competitive dynamics with OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon, and privacy concerns around facial recognition in wearable devices.

videos.title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates how to run two types of AI agents locally: Odysseus (a workspace agent) for task orchestration and OpenCode (a build agent) for code generation. The setup uses Gemma 4 via Ollama for local inference with Venice API as fallback, deployed in Docker for privacy-focused local execution.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard from Google Chrome that replaces DOM parsing and screenshot-based navigation with structured, named tools that agents call directly. It offers both declarative HTML attribute APIs and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering custom agent-callable actions, currently in early preview on Chrome 146.

videos.title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
videos.summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard protocol that enables AI agents and applications to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common architecture. MCP uses JSON-RPC for client-server communication and supports both local and remote connections via STDIO or HTTP transports.

videos.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser describes a four-layer system for asynchronous AI-assisted development that keeps humans available while agents work autonomously. The stack includes signal agents for context management, verification gates for safety, self-improvement loops via conversation history analysis, and holistic monitoring like sleep tracking to prevent burnout.

videos.title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
videos.summary: Groq is a hardware chip specifically designed for LLM inference. Jonathan Ross, the founder, discusses Groq's LPU architecture, its comparison to GPUs via Pareto curves and cost-per-token metrics, and a strategic agreement with NVIDIA. The interview covers technical aspects like static scheduling and mixture-of-experts models.

videos.title: AI That’s Too Dangerous For You? What we learned from S.A.T.A.N
videos.summary: IBM Technology discusses how AI accelerates the detection of zero-day vulnerabilities and reshapes cybersecurity defense strategies. The video argues that AI-driven vulnerability scanning combined with DevSecOps practices could improve system security despite the risks of AI capabilities.

videos.title: The AI Skill I use to prevent refactors
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery discusses an AI skill used to prevent code refactors. The full video is available at the provided link.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 in three variants: Nano, Super, and Ultra. The models combine hybrid mamba transformer, latent mixture of experts, and MTP in their architecture to differentiate from other open-source models.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is here!
videos.summary: A review of Claude Fable 5 (also called Claude Mythos 5) comparing it to GPT 5.5. The video demonstrates capabilities across vision tasks, 3D reconstruction, code generation, scientific applications, and benchmarks, with discussion of model specifications and performance characteristics.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that enables rapid building and deployment of production-grade AI agents. It provides agent identity management, sandboxed code execution, audit trails, and integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude Code.

videos.title: I One-Shotted An Entire App With Claude Fable 5 (Full Breakdown)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates building a video editing app in one hour by feeding Claude Fable 5 only screenshots of a SaaS landing page via Claude Code. The model successfully generated functional code including filler removal, clip detection, and subtitle export, though Fable 5 consumes API limits 2x faster than Opus and will be discontinued on June 22.

videos.title: Build a calendar app in AI Studio in 6 minutes
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech tutorial demonstrating how to build a no-code calendar app using Google AI Studio and Gemini. The app extracts travel itineraries and converts them into calendar invitations using advanced reasoning capabilities.

videos.title: Don't use Fable 5 in Claude… do this instead
videos.summary: David Ondrej advises against using Fable 5 with Claude, recommending an alternative approach instead. The video promotes his coding with AI community and mentions AutoGit as a related tool.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
videos.summary: A 4B parameter model finetuned with RL to use a get_table_name tool and self-correct SQL queries outperforms a 235B model that hallucinates answers. The technique emphasizes tool discipline and behavioral training over model scaling, achieving results on FinQA with training costs under 500 dollars.

videos.title: The Problem Solvers | Michael Truell at Cursor
videos.summary: Cursor is an AI-native coding platform that helps engineers and less technical builders ship code through agents. Founded by Michael Truell, the company has grown from 15 to 700 people and serves over 60% of Fortune 500 companies. Cursor has maintained a close partnership with Anthropic and Claude.

videos.title: PAI: The Life OS for AI-Powered Development
videos.summary: PAI (Personal AI Infrastructure) is a Life OS built on Claude Code that provides AI agents with persistent memory, custom skills, structured workflows, and a seven-phase algorithm for development tasks. It addresses the problem of AI forgetting project context, architecture, coding standards, and past decisions across sessions.

videos.title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemma 4's 31B model ranks fourth on the LM Arena leaderboard while being at least twice smaller than comparable competitors, running on a single GPU. The Apache 2.0 license shift enables sovereign institutions to deploy it without lengthy procurement review, with variants (2B, 4B, E2B, E4B) supporting deployment from mobile phones to hospital infrastructure.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Design – Full AI Design Workflow 2026
videos.summary: Claude Design is a tool that automates design workflows by generating branded landing pages and design assets from scratch. It replaces Figma for many tasks and operates through a 4-step framework involving design systems, generation, edits, and deployment.

videos.title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
videos.summary: PostHog is developing an autonomous agent pipeline that ingests product signals like rage clicks, error spikes, and Slack messages, clusters them using embedded LLM queries, and automatically generates pull requests with fixes. The system groups signals by semantic meaning rather than surface similarity and prioritizes actionable signals like error tracking over less specific ones.

videos.title: AI agents explained: Build your first agent in 8 minutes
videos.summary: A tutorial demonstrating how to build autonomous, self-correcting multi-agent systems using Google's Agent Development Kit. The video covers the ReAct Framework, three agent patterns (sequential, reactive, planning), and walks through building a practical blog writing agent with validation and auto-correction capabilities.

videos.title: The One Mistake Everyone Makes with AI
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery presents a video identifying a widespread mistake people make when working with AI. The full content appears to cover best practices or common pitfalls in AI application.

videos.title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
videos.summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast explores social engineering attacks against AI systems, including prompt injection vulnerabilities and self-replicating AI worms. Discusses whether AI agents are more vulnerable than humans and examines the emerging challenge of securing nonhuman identities in enterprise environments.

videos.title: Anthropic Just Dropped Fable 5 And It’s Terrifying
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, a Mythos-class AI model with advanced capabilities in software engineering, knowledge work, vision, analytics, scientific research, and cybersecurity. The company implemented safety systems to limit capabilities in high-risk domains including cybersecurity, biology, chemistry, and model distillation.

videos.title: Apple’s New Siri AI Is Now Gemini
videos.summary: Apple unveiled a rebuilt Siri AI at WWDC 2026 with personal context, screen awareness, Visual Intelligence, and cross-device integration, powered by Google Gemini. The announcement received poor investor reaction, stock fell, and EU regulatory restrictions delayed launch in those markets.

videos.title: Claude Mythos is FINALLY here (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Mythos, described as the company's first Mythos class model, which achieves top performance on multiple benchmarks including SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code. The model demonstrates capabilities in memory, long-running tasks, vision, website testing, fullstack development, and CAD.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 beats Pokémon FireRed only using vision
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 completed Pokémon FireRed using only vision-based input from raw game screenshots, without maps or additional game-state information. This represents improved multimodal capability over earlier Claude models that required helper harnesses for the same task.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses strategies for tech job hunting in an AI-driven market, emphasizing networking, personal branding, and open source contributions. The video argues that junior developers remain valuable despite AI automation and uses transportation industry evolution as an analogy for job market transformation.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

videos.title: Claude just dropped UltraCode... Unlock SuperPowers
videos.summary: Claude released UltraCode, a feature that enables spinning up to 10 parallel agents managed by code instead of a master LLM. The video demonstrates using UltraCode for judge panels, adversarial verification, deterministic fan-out patterns, and building an agentic operating system with dynamic model selection via OpenRouter.

videos.title: Build an app from your phone in AI Studio
videos.summary: Google AI Studio enables users to build custom applications from a mobile browser using voice commands and APIs like Nano Banana for image generation. The platform generates code in real time without requiring software installation, allowing deployment from anywhere.

videos.title: Behind the Scenes - Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 1 - Playing
videos.summary: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science course taught by David J. Malan. This video is a behind-the-scenes chapter from Brian Yu's Introduction to Artificial Intelligence segment, focusing on game-playing AI. The course is available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and as open courseware.

videos.title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: Flash is a deployment tool that abstracts GPU cloud provisioning through a Python decorator, enabling hot reload and direct IDE deployment. It simplifies the iteration cycle from commit-push-build-pull-allocate to a single line of code.

videos.title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
videos.summary: Military historian Sarah Paine outlines a two-thousand-year geopolitical framework dividing continental empires that pursue security through territorial conquest from maritime powers that pursue prosperity through trade. The framework explains why nations like China and Russia differ fundamentally in their strategic objectives from Britain and the US, with implications for modern conflicts.

videos.title: Claude Mythos 5 + Fable 5 Are Here And The Numbers Are INSANE
videos.summary: Anthropic released the Claude Mythos 5 model family with Fable 5 (a version with guardrails) now generally available. Fable 5 is included at no extra cost on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans through June 22, after which it will require usage credits pending capacity availability.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 plays Factorio
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 autonomously plays Factorio, a factory-building game, strategizing and constructing an automated factory without human intervention.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 simulates the solar system and predicts a solar eclipse
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 built a simulation of the solar system by deriving planetary orbital motion from physics first principles and used it to predict solar eclipses. This demonstrates the model's reasoning and simulation capabilities.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 sets a fluid simulation to Beethoven
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 generated both a fluid simulation and an EDM remix of Beethoven synchronized to the simulation motion. The model produced music through code without prior audio experience.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 designs a 3D-printable model in a Claude-built CAD editor
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 created a browser-based CAD editor that enables 3D-printable model design. The editor includes an integrated AI copilot built by Fable 5 that performs the actual modeling tasks.

videos.title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
videos.summary: Kuba Rogut argues that RAG is not dead but evolving beyond simple vector search into agentic retrieval combining vector search, full text search, regex, and filters. Cursor's semantic search approach shows 24% accuracy gains and 2.6% code retention improvements, while Google search volume for RAG increased dramatically in mid-2025, contradicting the "RAG is dead" discourse.

videos.title: GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening with cuML and Agent Platform
videos.summary: Google Cloud and NVIDIA demonstrate GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening using cuML and the Agent Platform. The solution achieves 20x-45x training speedups on tabular data for molecular predictions like lung cancer binding likelihood, scaling to trillion-molecule screening without rewriting existing pandas or scikit-learn code.

videos.title: From Transcription to Live Music: Gemini's Audio Stack — Thor Schaeff, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemini 3 Flash Preview enables audio understanding with speaker labels, timestamps, emotion tags, language detection, and summarization in a single API call. Gemini 3.1 Flash Live is a real-time multimodal sound-to-sound model with integrated reasoning. Lyria 3 is a music generation model capable of producing full songs with lyrics and can be called via tool use by Gemini Live.

videos.title: Stop Prompting Claude. Use Karpathy's Method Instead.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese describes a three-layer methodology attributed to Andrej Karpathy for using Claude more effectively, arguing that most users prompt Claude incorrectly. The method claims to enable 10x faster development with Claude Code.

videos.title: How To Design a Complete Brand Identity With AI (Best AI Logo Maker)
videos.summary: Design.com is an AI platform that automates brand identity design including logo generation, brand kit creation, and template application. It enables users to create logos with plain-English edits, auto-apply brand colors across 500,000+ templates, and generate animated logos for social media within minutes without requiring design software.

videos.title: Introducing Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
videos.summary: Google DeepMind introduces Gemini 3.5 Live Translate, a speech-to-speech translation model supporting 70+ languages with automatic language detection and natural-sounding dubbing. The model is available via the Gemini API, AI Studio, Google Translate apps, and Google Meet private preview.

videos.title: Kimi Code is good at video reasoning with Kimi K2.6 🎬
videos.summary: Kimi Code is a video reasoning tool built on Kimi K2.6 that accepts reference videos and generates .cube LUT (color lookup table) files based on visual queries about colors, shots, or visual style. Users can drag in video references and request specific visual attributes to generate ready-to-use color grading outputs.

videos.title: How I'd Learn AI From Scratch in 2026 (skip the useless 80%)
videos.summary: Jeff Su presents a prioritized learning strategy for AI in 2026 centered on picking one model to master, building practical context through real projects, and assembling AI systems with tools. The guidance dismisses rote prompting frameworks in favor of deeper model understanding and contextual work.

videos.title: Finally… A NotebookLM Alternative With an API
videos.summary: Open Notebook is a self-hosted, open-source alternative to Google NotebookLM that provides an API and more control over document and research workflows. The video reviews its capabilities including source ingestion, grounded question answering, and podcast generation from documents.

videos.title: This is why my AI Agents never guess
videos.summary: A video from JavaScript Mastery discussing design principles for AI agents that avoid guessing or unreliable outputs. The creator shares techniques for building more deterministic and reliable AI agent systems.

videos.title: Building AI Agent Systems and Scaling Challenges in Agentic AI
videos.summary: Scaling AI agent systems introduces cost, latency, and failure risks that require careful attention to multi-agent design, coordination, and system architecture. The video discusses why naive scaling approaches break and how to address real-world scalability constraints.

videos.title: Code with Claude 2026 | Tokyo
videos.summary: Anthropic held Code with Claude 2026 in Tokyo, a conference featuring announcements about Claude models, the Claude Platform, and Claude Code, with demonstrations from partner organizations Canva, Mizuho, and NRI.

videos.title: Gemma Playground: Robot Duck
videos.summary: Xavier Plantaz demonstrates the Open Duck Mini v2 project running Gemma 4 E2B on Raspberry Pi 5 and Jetson Orin Nano. The on-device stack uses Parakeet for speech-to-text, Gemma 4 for inference, and Kokoro for text-to-speech, enabling real-time multimodal interaction with robotic ducks.

videos.title: New BEST local AI image generator is here!
videos.summary: Ideogram 4 is presented as an open-source local AI image generator with capabilities including world knowledge, text rendering, prompt adherence, bounding box control, and comics/manga generation. The video demonstrates integration with ComfyUI and compares it as the best available option for local image generation.

videos.title: 2026 AI Engineer Vibe Reel
videos.summary: AI Engineer is promoting the World's Fair conference in San Francisco scheduled for June 29 to July 2, 2026. The announcement directs to ai.engineer/wf for tickets and schedule information.

videos.title: Graphify + Obsidian + Claude Code = CHEAT CODE
videos.summary: Chase AI demonstrates a workflow combining Graphify for repository analysis, Obsidian for knowledge management, and Claude Code for code generation. The integration merges knowledge graph insights into Obsidian vaults to improve Claude Code's answers.

videos.title: ChatGPT Is Getting Its Biggest Upgrade Ever
videos.summary: OpenAI is reportedly planning to transform ChatGPT from a chatbot into a full AI super app integrating Codex, agents, image tools, third-party integrations, and workplace automation. This expansion reflects broader competition in enterprise AI as competitors like Anthropic advance their own offerings.

videos.title: Working Like a Lawyer with Claude
videos.summary: Mark Pike from Anthropic and Anna Gressel from Freshfields discuss how lawyers are using Claude. They explore how legal work is changing with AI and emphasize that final judgment calls remain with the lawyer.

videos.title: Intro to multi-agent systems with ADK
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech introduces the Agent Development Kit, a framework that simplifies building and testing agents with custom tools and debugging capabilities. The tutorial demonstrates creating multi-agent systems with real-time data retrieval and Google Search integration to reduce hallucinations.

videos.title: Claude Code + Graphify = Insane Agentic OS
videos.summary: Graphify creates a knowledge graph representation of a project that allows Claude Code to query project structure without rescanning the entire repository each session. This reduces token costs and improves accuracy. The tool can be integrated into an agentic operating system alongside Claude, Hermes, and AntiGravity.

videos.title: Road to 5 Million Tokens: Breaking Barriers in Long Context Training — Max Ryabinin, Together AI
videos.summary: Max Ryabinin presents a stack of techniques for training LLMs with very long context windows, culminating in Untied Ulysses, a refinement of context parallelism that chunks attention heads and reuses buffers to reduce activation memory with minimal throughput cost. The approach successfully trains models up to 5 million tokens on commodity hardware like 8xH100 nodes.

videos.title: Reflecting on a year of Claude Code
videos.summary: Claude Code, an agentic coding tool that runs in the terminal, marked its one-year anniversary of general availability. The video features reflections from Boris Cherny and Cat Wu on adoption patterns, verification practices, auto mode capabilities, and emerging use cases beyond engineering teams.

videos.title: AI YouTube Automation: How to Start a YouTube Channel With AI
videos.summary: Arthur Winer demonstrates a full AI-driven YouTube video production workflow from ideation through publishing in 20 minutes. The tutorial covers idea research via ChatGPT, script generation with Claude, thumbnail creation with GPT-4 Vision, voice cloning, AI avatar generation via HeyGen, B-roll synthesis, editing with Descript, and repurposing to Shorts with Opus Clip.

videos.title: Simulating Percolation in Python: How Do Wildfires & Diseases Spread?
videos.summary: This video tutorial demonstrates how to implement percolation simulations in Python to model the spread of wildfires and diseases. Percolation theory is used to understand how phenomena propagate through networks and systems.

videos.title: Did Apple (Finally) Get AI Right At WWDC?, Anthropic’s Worry, Microsoft vs. OpenAI
videos.summary: A weekly tech news discussion covering Apple's AI integration at WWDC, Anthropic's claims about AI self-improvement, and the emerging competition between Microsoft and OpenAI. The hosts debate whether these developments represent genuine progress or marketing, and discuss tensions between safety and commercial pressures in AI companies.

videos.title: Inside Google Antigravity 2.0: The complete developer guide | The Agent Factory
videos.summary: Google Antigravity 2.0 is an AI agent development platform that evolved from an IDE into a standalone ecosystem with desktop agent manager, CLI, SDK, and custom IDE. It enables developers to use voice prompts to orchestrate parallel sub-agents for code generation, testing, and deployment tasks while managing code quality through skill-based context compression.

videos.title: Hermes Agent is crazy… 180,000+ github stars
videos.summary: Hermes Agent is a GitHub project with over 180,000 stars. The content demonstrates how to run Hermes Agent with MiniMax m3 model to reduce costs by approximately 100x.

videos.title: How A Beauty Company Built An AI Agent — With Rachel Williamson and Josh Siebert
videos.summary: Ulta Beauty deployed ServiceNow's AI-powered HR platform to automate workforce operations across 65,000 employees. The interview covers decisions about automation boundaries, human-in-the-loop design, personalization in employee interactions, and impacts on hiring and onboarding processes.

videos.title: Why More Context Makes Your Agent Dumber and What to Do About It — Nupur Sharma, Qodo
videos.summary: Nupur Sharma discusses the U-curve problem where agents mishandle middle context in large input windows. She covers iterative retrieval, hierarchical summarization, and self-correction as solutions, then introduces an 80/20 orchestration split using high-reasoning models for discovery and lighter deterministic models for validation, demonstrated in Qodo's code review system.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early users are not proof of real product-market fit and that founders often mistake enthusiasm for actual demand. He contends that true PMF manifests as pull (customers asking prices) rather than push (requests to stay updated), and recommends embedded customer engagement over formal interviews to assess market validation.

videos.title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare
videos.summary: Eval++ is Cloudflare's compute primitive for AI agents, built on Durable Objects for addressable, persistent, stateful execution. Dynamic Workers enable sandboxed execution of LLM-generated code with explicit capability grants, eliminating distributed systems complexity for features like resumable streaming and multi-tab sync.

videos.title: Headroom: The Netflix Tool That Makes AI Agents 10x Cheaper
videos.summary: Headroom is an open-source tool that compresses inputs to LLMs using content-type-aware compressors and locally trained models, reducing token usage by 60-95%. Built by a Netflix engineer, it keeps compression local and uses breadcrumb hashing for on-demand retrieval of full original content.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a large language model that adopts sparse attention (MSA) instead of full attention for improved inference efficiency. The model includes optimized KV cache handling and tiled I/O improvements that reduce latency during prefill and decode stages.

videos.title: What Is Agentic Coding? How AI Agents Modernize Code
videos.summary: IBM Technology explains agentic coding and its application to legacy code modernization. The video positions AI agents as accelerators for software development that reduce risk and improve DevSecOps outcomes.

videos.title: I Turned Cheap Cloud Storage Into a 1PB Local Drive (With JuiceFS)
videos.summary: JuiceFS is a POSIX-compliant virtual file system that layers cloud object storage like AWS S3 with a multi-tiered caching engine using local NVMe for performance. The video demonstrates setting up JuiceFS with Redis metadata, configuring local SSD cache, and running AI inference workloads directly against cloud buckets while monitoring performance via Prometheus and Better Stack observability.

videos.title: Start a 1-Person AI Creative Agency with Higgsfield + Claude
videos.summary: Liam Ottley demonstrates how to build a one-person AI creative agency using Higgsfield for image generation, Claude for automation and copywriting, Notion for client databases, and Apify for competitor scraping. The system produces product photography, UGC ads, commercials, and ad copy while automatically tracking performance and competitor data.

videos.title: the most POWERFUL claude code feature in months
videos.summary: Graphify is a non-RAG approach for analyzing large code bases by creating knowledge graphs to store extracted information. It enables more accurate responses with reduced token overhead compared to traditional methods.

videos.title: Did Google Just Fall Behind Again?, WWDC Preview, Anthropic Files To Go Public

The conversation covers AI agents reshaping web and app usage, Apple's WWDC, and Anthropic's IPO filing, framing these as major power shifts in tech.

videos.title: Harness Engineering Is AI’s New Gold Rush
videos.summary: Harness engineering focuses on the infrastructure surrounding AI models including tools, memory, context, permissions, verification, recovery paths, and feedback loops to make AI agents reliable. As foundation models converge in capability, competitive advantage shifts from prompt engineering to the broader harness system that converts raw model intelligence into dependable operational behavior.

videos.title: Big Tech Is Buying Up Open Source... Vite Is Next

videos.title: LLM Observability, Evaluation, Experimentation Platform — Dat Ngo, Arize

The enterprise product includes an AI layer called Alex that automatically scans traces, surfaces anomalies, and generates evaluations to reduce manual observability work.

videos.title: Under 5 minutes to a deployed LLM endpoint — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: RunPod is a serverless LLM deployment platform that enables developers to deploy models on H100 GPUs in under five minutes, with pay-per-request pricing. Founded in 2022 from repurposed crypto mining GPUs, the platform now serves 500,000 developers with $120 million in annual recurring revenue.

videos.title: From MCP to Scale: Pipelines That Build Themselves — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Rafael Levi demonstrates how Bright Data's Model Context Protocol integrates with agents to automate web scraper generation, maintenance, and repair. The system uses agents to inspect site structures, generate production scrapers, and self-repair pipelines when websites change.

videos.title: Reinventing Entropy | Compression is Intelligence Part 1
videos.summary: 3Blue1Brown's video explores the mathematical foundations of entropy and compression, presenting visual intuitions for how entropy quantifies information content and how compressibility relates to intelligence. The video references Claude Shannon's foundational work on information theory and demonstrates these concepts through language compressibility examples.

videos.title: Google's New AI Architecture Changes Everything (Gemma 4 12B)

The architecture eliminates intermediate encoder models, enabling faster offline performance on consumer hardware like Apple Silicon.

videos.title: Text Diffusion — Brendon O'Donoghue, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Text Diffusion is a generative technique that uses iterative denoising steps instead of autoregressive token prediction. Brendon O'Donoghue from Google DeepMind explains that diffusion models with bidirectional attention can self-correct by seeing future tokens, require roughly 10x fewer memory transfers than autoregressive generation, but currently have lower throughput at scale compared to traditional language models.

videos.title: Late Interaction Basics | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Late interaction is a search paradigm that preserves token-level representations while enabling pre-computation of documents, contrasted against single-vector embeddings and cross-encoders. The video explains how late interaction models like ColBERT balance retrieval speed with relevance precision for large-scale collections.

videos.title: Use Cases for Multi-Vector Search | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Lesson comparing single-vector and multi-vector search approaches using BGE versus ColBERT on technical support queries. Multi-vector search with token-level verification prevents false positives from keyword-stuffed content by requiring strong matches at the token level rather than just topical overlap.

videos.title: Problems of Multi-Vector Search | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Multi-vector search improves retrieval quality but requires brute-force scanning because HNSW indexing fails with MaxSim due to its asymmetric, query-dependent nature. Storing hundreds of token embeddings per document incurs roughly 167x more memory overhead than single-vector approaches, creating significant computational and storage costs at scale.

videos.title: Multi-Vector Embeddings in Qdrant | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Tutorial on configuring Qdrant for multi-vector search using the MaxSim comparator, indexing documents with FastEmbed local inference, and querying with ColBERT embeddings. Covers collection setup, HNSW disabling for MaxSim compatibility, and on-disk vector storage for memory-constrained environments.

videos.title: How ColPali Models Work | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: ColPali extends late interaction from text to visual documents by using a Vision Language Model (PaliGemma-3B) to divide document images into a 32x32 grid of patches, each producing visual token embeddings. Text queries match directly against these visual patches using MaxSim scoring without requiring OCR.

videos.title: ColPali Family Overview | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: The ColPali family includes compact embedding models (ColSmol, ColFlor, ColModernVBERT) for visual document retrieval with reduced parameters compared to the base ColPali. Multilingual variants from NVIDIA and Nomic AI are available, and ViDoRe V3 serves as the benchmark for evaluating visual retrieval models. Qdrant is a vector search engine in Rust supporting semantic and hybrid search.

videos.title: Visual Interpretability of ColPali | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Qdrant demonstrates visual interpretability for ColPali embeddings, which represent documents as 1,024 patch embeddings mapped to spatial locations. The system computes per-token similarity heatmaps overlaid on documents to show which regions match query terms, enabling interpretable multi-vector search and relevance debugging.

videos.title: Multi-Stage Retrieval with Universal Query API | Qdrant Multi-Vector Search
videos.summary: Qdrant's prefetch plus rerank pattern uses fast initial retrieval (single-vector or BM25) to narrow candidates down from millions to hundreds, then applies ColBERT reranking to refine results. The technique emphasizes oversampling in stage one to ensure sufficient candidates for effective reranking.

videos.title: CS50 for Business - Lecture 8 - Securing Systems
videos.summary: CS50 for Business Lecture 8 covers systems security fundamentals including strong passwords, encryption, two-factor authentication, and best practices to protect data from cyber threats. This is the final lecture in the CS50 for Business course series taught by David J. Malan at Harvard University.

videos.title: How (and why) to take a logarithm of an image
videos.summary: 3Blue1Brown demonstrates how logarithmic transformations of complex images reveal underlying mathematical structure, using Escher's Print Gallery as a case study. The video explores conformal maps, complex exponentials, and logarithms to explain the geometric properties that make the artwork's self-similar spiral pattern possible.

videos.title: How to Build & Sell AI Agents in 2026: Ultimate Beginner’s Guide
videos.summary: A comprehensive tutorial on building and selling AI agents using n8n without coding experience. The guide covers four real-world AI agents including a Telegram expense tracker, website lead generation chatbot, voice agent with Retell AI, and a sales copilot with Lovable frontend, along with monetization strategies for launching an AI automation agency.

videos.title: Choosing the Wrong Language Costs AI Teams Months of Work
videos.summary: Darren Builds AI argues that programming language selection significantly affects AI team productivity, particularly as teams scale. The video advocates for TypeScript over Python, citing benefits of language server protocols, type checking, and compile-time error detection to reduce runtime mistakes in AI coding.

videos.title: CS50 for Business - Lecture 7 - Deploying Databases
videos.summary: CS50 for Business Lecture 7 covers deploying and managing databases at scale using SQL and NoSQL technologies. It teaches best practices for efficient data storage and querying. The course is available through edX, Harvard Extension School, and Harvard Summer School.

videos.title: If You Use Agent Zero, This Video is for You (EPIC FAIL)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs documents a failed attempt to build an AI ad campaign generator using Agent Zero, encountering persistent LiteLLM errors that made the framework unusable. He concludes that Agent Zero shows promise but advises testing thoroughly before committing production workflows, noting Claude Code or OpenClaw would have completed the task.

videos.title: Claude Channels Just Dropped, And It Kills OpenClaw (Again)
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Channels, a new feature that encapsulates functionality previously available in OpenClaw. The feature allows users to set up integrated workflows across Discord, Telegram, and other platforms within Claude Code, demonstrated with setup guidance across multiple communication channels.

videos.title: This NotebookLM feature cuts my workload by 50%
videos.summary: Jeff Su demonstrates NotebookLM's mind map feature, which generates visual topic hierarchies from uploaded documents. Users can selectively explore branches of interest rather than reviewing all source material, reducing preparation workload for content projects.

videos.title: COLD EMAIL COPYWRITING & OUTREACH Full Course 2026
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's "Cold Email Copywriting & Outreach Full Course 2026" is a multi-hour educational program covering the psychology of persuasion, copywriting frameworks, offer construction, platform-specific optimization (LinkedIn, X, SMS, Instagram), email subject lines, follow-ups, and AI integration. The course includes real-world cold email analysis and advanced outbound techniques.

videos.title: Claude Dispatch Just Dropped, And It Kills OpenClaw
videos.summary: Claude Dispatch is Anthropic's new feature for agentic workflows through the Claude app. It provides a more secure alternative to OpenClaw by running autonomous tasks directly within Claude rather than through third-party platforms like Telegram.

videos.title: I Set Up a Claude Code Operating System for 6 Founders in 8 Hours
videos.summary: Liam Ottley demonstrates setting up Claude Code as an AI Operating System for six non-technical founders in a single day. The setup includes a Context OS with four context files teaching Claude about business operations, enabling non-coders to build automation for invoicing, finance reporting, competitor analysis, and content workflows.

videos.title: NotebookLM Changed Completely: Here's What Matters (in 2026)
videos.summary: Jeff Su reviews NotebookLM's major 2026 updates including a three-column layout and Studio panel tools. He ranks tools into Tier 1 (Reports, Slide Decks, Infographics, Mind Maps) and Tier 2 (Data Tables, Video Overviews, Quiz, Flashcards, Audio Overviews) categories. He demonstrates workflows for turning source documents into finished reports, presentations, and social-ready content.

videos.title: How to Set Up and Use SpaceBot (Better Than OpenClaw?)
videos.summary: SpaceBot is an open-source framework designed for building AI agents integrated with community platforms like Discord, Telegram, Slack, Twitch, and WebChat. The tutorial demonstrates Docker installation, model routing for token efficiency, and multi-agent capabilities with features including memory, cron jobs, and platform bindings.

videos.title: Claude Code + Nano Banana 2 + Kling =  $15K Animated Sites
videos.summary: Video demonstrates a technique for rapidly building high-end animated websites using Claude Code for development, Kling video generation, and Nano Banana for image synthesis. The creator combines Leon Lin's taste skill with generative tools to produce custom websites reportedly worth $15K in a streamlined workflow.

videos.title: Stop Fixing Your Claude Skills. Autoresearch Does It For You
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to automatically improve Claude Code skills by combining Karpathy's autoresearch principles with evals. The approach provides a step-by-step walkthrough for practitioners to optimize their own LLM-based skills through automated evaluation and refinement.

videos.title: How to Make Your AI Agents Work Better (With Context Hygiene)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs presents context hygiene as a technique for maintaining AI agent performance across multiple messages and conversations. The approach involves three layers, Skills, Harnesses, and Hygiene, along with practices like lazy loading and multi-agent orchestration to keep context windows clean and avoid re-explaining requirements.

videos.title: NotebookLM is now bigger than Gemini
videos.summary: NotebookLM now has higher search interest than Google Gemini. Its core strength is the ability to ingest multi-format documents and provide grounded, accurate answers without hallucinations. It is recommended when accuracy matters and source documents in various formats need to be consulted.

videos.title: 10 years of AlphaGo: The turning point for AI | Thore Graepel & Pushmeet Kohli
videos.summary: A Google DeepMind retrospective marking ten years since AlphaGo defeated Lee Sedol at Go in March 2016. The speakers argue AlphaGo was a foundational moment that sparked the modern AI revolution and enabled subsequent breakthroughs in algorithmic discovery and scientific challenges like protein folding.

videos.title: What Is an AI Operating System? (And Why Every Business Will Need One)
videos.summary: Liam Ottley outlines AI Operating System (AIOS) as a layered methodology for businesses to shift focus from operational tasks to strategic growth. AIOS emphasizes automation and augmentation through five layers built around existing business models, with success measured by away-from-desk autonomy, task automation percentage, and revenue per employee.
